title: iris humilis subsp. orientalis
intro: Iris humilis subsp. orientalis, also known as the Turkish iris, is a subspecies of the Iris humilis plant, which belongs to the iris family. It is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia, known for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparations of Iris humilis subsp. orientalis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory problems and skin irritations.
